items.title: Man kills parents, brother in ‘very bloody’ attack in Sydney home, police allege
items.standfirst: A 32-year-old man has been charged after three people died in an attack at a home in Rosemeadow.
items.content: A 32-year-old man has been charged after three people died in an attack at a home in Rosemeadow.

items.title: Man arrested after three family members killed in attack in Sydney home
items.standfirst: The three victims are all from the same family, with one survivinf brother calling police to a "grizzly" scene.
items.content: A man is in custody after three of his family members died following an alleged attack in their home in Sydney's south-west overnight.The mother, father and brother of the arrested man were killed, while another brother was injured as emergency services were confronted by a "very grizzly crime scene". The surviving brother, 30, was able to call police, who arrived alongside paramedics to a house on Juliet Close in Rosemeadow just after 1.30am.READ MORE: Murder charges laid after death of five-year-old girl in NT They found a 65-year-old woman inside the house, but despite the efforts of paramedics, she died at the scene.After a search of the house and adjoining granny flat, a 25-year-old man was found dead, and a 64-year-old male was found with serious head injuries.He was taken to Liverpool Hospital, but died of his injuries later this morning. The surviving man was taken to hospital, but has since been released. WORLD: Donald Trump says he has received Iranian peace proposal to end warSuperintendent Grant Healey said emergency services were faced with a "horrendous" scene."They were confronted with a very bloody scene," he said, confirming the victims showed signs of suffering from blunt force trauma injuries as well as injuries caused by an edged bladed weapon." The police and ambulance did a fantastic job in very trying circumstances."Healy said no weapons had been recovered, but that police were still investigating an "extensive" crime scene.WATCH: Two charged after Melbourne restaurant torched in arson attackAt about 2.30am, a silver sedan arrived at the home, and police arrested the driver, a 32-year-old man.He is related to all of the people found inside the house, with the two people in their 60s being his parents, and the two other men being his brothers. The 32-year-old was taken to Campbelltown Police Station, where he is assisting police. He has not been charged with any offences. READ MORE: Scammers attempt to extort Sydney removalist with sudden flurry of negative reviews Healey said police were still piecing together where the man was between police arriving on the scene and his return to the house. He said the family was not well known to police, especially for domestic violence incidents."The family weren't known to us, this is a very tragic incident, there was no way for us to predict this kind of thing to occur," he said.A crime scene has been established, with officers connected to the Homicide Squad beginning an investigation.A report will be prepared for the coroner.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Victorian Liberals hold onto Nepean after gliding to by-election victory
items.standfirst: The by-election had been regarded as Liberal Leader Jess Wilson's first test ahead of the November state election.
items.content: T he Victorian Liberal Party has hung on to the seat of Nepean in the first big leadership test for Opposition Leader Jess Wilson ahead of the November state election.Despite a challenge from One Nation and an Independent, Liberal candidate Anthony Marsh rose to the top of the ballot after more than 50,000 locals cast their votes on the Mornington Peninsula yesterday.The former Mornington Shire Council Mayor succeeds former deputy Liberal leader and ex-pro tennis player Sam Groth, who retired from politics in February.READ MORE: Two charged after Melbourne restaurant torched in arson attack With almost 80 per cent of the vote now counted, Marsh has picked up 38.5 per cent of the first preference vote.That represents an almost 10 per cent slump in first preference support for the Liberals compared with the last election.Most of those votes went to One Nation candidate Darren Hercus and Independent Tracee Hutchison, who each saw first preference swings of more than 20 per cent towards them.But it wasn't enough to tip the Liberals from the seat, who led by more than 10,000 votes once preferences were distributed."It is the honour of my life to be standing here in front of you as the next member for Nepean," Marsh told Liberal Party supporters after a clear result was declared around 9pm."It's been a hard-fought campaign, I think we're day 67 in and to stand in a room full of blue after what was an incredible day, I am so honoured and privileged again to be in this role."READ MORE: Jefferson Lewis charged with murder after death of five year-old girl The by-election had been regarded as Wilson's first test ahead of the state election in November."I say thank you to the residents down here on the peninsula for putting your trust in Anthony and in me," Wilson told supporters last night."I will not let you down, I will fight for you every day."I have met thousands of you over the past few weeks. I have looked you in the eye and I have said 'as your next premier, I will deliver for you'. "BREAKING NEWS: Man arrested after three people die in attack in Sydney homeThe by-election was also touted as a major litmus test for One Nation, which has gained a large groundswell of voter support in the last six months owing to a strong appetite for change.Pauline Hanson is yet to comment on last night's performance, but data shows around one in four Nepean voters put One Nation first on their ballot yesterday.Federal counterparts in Farrer were watching the vote closely ahead of their own by-election next weekend. Speaking to Weekend Today, former Nationals leader David Littleproud said it was difficult to draw comparisons from the strong Liberal result in Nepean."It will be a challenge, I think federal and state issues are different," Littleproud said of the Farrer by-election."By-elections send very different messages to general elections, but every election sends a very strong message for everyone to learn from, as it was for the Liberals in this state seat as well."NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
